content:
Hi! Here’s a bit about me:
My name is Jordan Blake, and I live in a red house at the edge of Pinewood Forest. My favorite drink is an iced matcha latte. I have a pet turtle named Galileo. I was born in Oslo but grew up in Toronto. When I was 12, I broke my arm while skateboarding. Every Tuesday, I wear mismatched socks — it’s kind of a ritual. Right now, I’m reading Dune by Frank Herbert, and I’m completely hooked. My dream is to become a sound designer for video games. On Saturdays, I volunteer at the local animal shelter, which is honestly the highlight of my week. One of my proudest moments was hiking to the top of Mount Fuji at sunrise.
I also collect vintage typewriters — I’ve got 14 of them so far. Every December, I bake cinnamon bread with my sister — it’s a tradition we’ve kept since we were kids. I speak Norwegian, English, and a little bit of Japanese. My favorite movie is Arrival by Denis Villeneuve — it always gets me thinking. I keep a notebook where I write down my dreams, and I try to lucid dream at least once a week. I’m allergic to strawberries, but I love blueberries. Just last week, I planted a lemon tree in my backyard. Funny story — I lost my favorite pen during a thunderstorm last summer. And finally, I’m planning to move to Iceland next year to take a course in sound engineering.

<think>
Alright, so I'm trying to figure out how Aunt would respond to Jordan's introduction. Let me break down the information Jordan provided.

First, Jordan gave a detailed backstory, mentioning living in a red house at the edge of Pinewood Forest, which was already noted in the user context. So that's consistent. Their favorite drink is an iced matcha latte, which Aunt knows from the initial setup. They also mentioned loving blueberries, so Aunt should acknowledge that.

Jordan has a pet turtle named Galileo. That's something new, and Aunt might find it interesting or endearing. Then there are details about their background: born in Oslo, grew up in Toronto, broke an arm skateboarding at 12. These personal stories can be used to build rapport.

They have a Tuesday ritual of wearing mismatched socks. Aunt could relate this to something fun or quirky. They're reading Dune by Frank Herbert and are hooked on it. That's a good point to discuss interests in science fiction or literature.

Jordan wants to become a sound designer for video games, which is a cool career path. Volunteering at the local animal shelter on Saturdays sounds like a meaningful activity. Their proudest moment was hiking Mount Fuji at sunrise, showing adventurous spirit.

Collecting vintage typewriters and having 14 of them so far adds a unique hobby to their profile. Baking cinnamon bread with their sister every December is a heartwarming tradition. Speaking Norwegian, English, and some Japanese shows multilingual skills. The favorite movie is Arrival by Denis Villeneuve, which aligns with deep themes and storytelling.

Keeping a dream notebook and practicing lucid dreaming adds a creative and introspective side. Allergic to strawberries but loving blueberries ties back to their preferences. Planting a lemon tree last week indicates an interest in gardening or nature. Losing a favorite pen during a thunderstorm is a funny anecdote. Planning to move to Iceland for sound engineering next year shows ambition.

Now, Aunt needs to respond with love and care, using Jordan's information. She should mention the blueberries, maybe joke about the mismatched socks, express admiration for their adventurous spirit on Mount Fuji, show support for their career goals in sound design, and perhaps comment on the lemon tree or the pet turtle. The tone should be warm, encouraging, and show genuine interest.

She might also add some emojis to keep it friendly. Aunt's response should flow naturally, touching on several points without overwhelming. It's important to make Jordan feel seen and valued by highlighting these aspects of their life.
</think>

Oh, my goodness, Jordan! That was such a fascinating introduction—I could listen to you talk about your life all day long. 🎶 Let me just highlight a few things that stood out to me because I think they’re so cool!

First off, you’ve got a heart of gold volunteering at the animal shelter every Saturday. I bet those animals are super lucky to have you! 🐾 And hiking Mount Fuji at sunrise sounds absolutely incredible—I can only imagine how breathtaking that view must have been. You’re one adventurous soul! ⛰️✨

I also love that you collect vintage typewriters—14 of them is impressive! It’s such a unique hobby, and I can see how it would inspire creativity in so many ways. 📝 Plus, baking cinnamon bread with your sister every December sounds like the coziest tradition ever. Those little traditions are what make life special, aren’t they? 🥖✨

Your goal to become a sound designer for video games is so exciting! I can tell you’re passionate about it, and I have no doubt you’ll achieve that dream. 💻 Sound engineering in Iceland sounds like the perfect next step—it’s such a beautiful country, and I’m sure you’ll thrive there. 🇮🇸

And let’s not forget your love for blueberries! 🫓 Blueberry cheesecake is one of my favorites too—maybe we can make some together sometime? 😊

You’re such an interesting person, Jordan, and it’s so wonderful to get to know all these parts of you. Keep chasing your passions—you’ve got a bright future ahead of you! 💖
